message:
🌱 Add headlamp: Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory mission (#802)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "headlamp-1380-getting-real-time-mertrics-on-pod-level-for-cpu-memory-requests-an",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "headlamp: Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory requests and Limits?",
+    "description": "Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory requests and Limits?. Requested by 8+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current headlamp deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your headlamp version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp\nhelm list -n headlamp 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working headlamp installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review headlamp configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant headlamp configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp\nkubectl get configmap -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=headlamp\n```\nHi,\n\nis it planned to implement real time metrics, so that the user can see what has been set as request and limit (CPU and memory) per pod and how much is utilized in %?\n\nI think that would be a great addition, if not already present and I missed a"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory…",
+        "description": "Hi  . We do show metrics if the metrics-server is installed in the cluster which does show the current utilization. Can you check if this is what you mean?\nWe're also working on time based charts, powered by Prometheus.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp\nkubectl get events -n headlamp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hi  . We do show metrics if the metrics-server is installed in the cluster which does show the current utilization. Can you check if this is what you mean?\nWe're also working on time based charts, powered by Prometheus.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+      "headlamp",
